commands.review_summary:
The PR changes macOS voice-send paths and gateway payload builders so nil thinking values are omitted instead of forcing `low` or `default`, with Swift tests updated around inherited thinking payloads.

PR surface: Other +69. Total +69 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: macOS push-to-talk reaches VoiceWakeForwarder, which currently sends explicit `low`, and the gateway rejects explicit unsupported thinking for Ollama off-only profiles. I did not run the macOS companion app or live Ollama path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The patch does not need automated repair, but merge should wait for contributor or maintainer macOS proof because the supplied commands did not execute the changed behavior.

target.title: fix #87768: [Bug]: push to talk mac os companion app hard codes thinking low
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop macOS voice forwarding from forcing `thinking: "low"` on push-to-talk/Voice Wake sends.
- Let `GatewayConnection` omit `thinking` when callers pass `nil`, so the gateway resolves the session/model-supported thinking policy instead of treating voice sends as an explicit override.
- Update macOS tests to expect inherited thinking for Voice Wake and gateway payloads.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** The macOS Voice Wake/push-to-talk forwarder always built `ForwardOptions` with `thinking: "low"`, and Talk Mode also passed `"low"` to `chat.send`. The gateway treats those fields as explicit thinking overrides, so an Ollama model profile that only supports `off` rejects the run before the dictated message reaches webchat.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The voice entry points now pass `nil` for thinking, and `GatewayConnection` omits the RPC `thinking` field when no override is present. That removes the unsupported explicit override at the source while keeping explicit UI/deep-link thinking choices intact.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium â€” source-level root cause and payload behavior are clear, but macOS Swift/live companion app proof is not available in this Linux environment.
- **Patch quality notes:** The production change is limited to the macOS gateway payload builder and the two voice send call sites; tests assert inherited thinking is represented by an omitted payload field.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The gateway remains the source of truth for session/model thinking resolution; the macOS app only stops forcing a caller override on voice sends.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** macOS push-to-talk and Talk Mode no longer send an explicit `low` thinking override, so Ollama/off-only model profiles can inherit gateway/session thinking resolution instead of failing on unsupported `low`.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux source checkout only. This environment has no Swift toolchain or macOS companion app runtime available.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git diff --check`; `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter VoiceWakeForwarderTests`; `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeRuntimeSpeechTests`; `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter GatewayConnectionControlTests`.
- **Evidence after fix:** After-fix terminal capture:

```text
$ git diff --check
exit=0
$ swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter VoiceWakeForwarderTests
/bin/bash: swift: æœªæ‰¾åˆ°å‘½ä»¤
exit=127
$ swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeRuntimeSpeechTests
/bin/bash: swift: æœªæ‰¾åˆ°å‘½ä»¤
exit=127
$ swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter GatewayConnectionControlTests
/bin/bash: swift: æœªæ‰¾åˆ°å‘½ä»¤
exit=127
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Static diff shows `VoiceWakeForwarder` defaults `thinking` to `nil`, selected forward options no longer pass `"low"`, Talk Mode calls `chatSend(... thinking: nil ...)`, and `GatewayConnection` omits `thinking` from agent/chat RPC params when no override is provided.
- **What was not tested:** macOS Swift test execution, a built companion app, and live macOS/Ollama push-to-talk behavior.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/VoiceWakeForwarderTests.swift`, `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/TalkModeRuntimeSpeechTests.swift`, and `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/GatewayConnectionControlTests.swift`.
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter VoiceWakeForwarderTests`
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeRuntimeSpeechTests`
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter GatewayConnectionControlTests`
